steps.title: Interpret rate as the slope of the cumulative-rainfall graph
steps.content: Because $y$ is total accumulated rainfall, the rate of rainfall during any interval equals the slope of the curve over that interval. A horizontal segment has slope $0$ (no new rainfall); a steeper segment means a higher rate.

steps.title: Read the segment from $x=2$ to $x=4$
steps.content:
On the graph, the curve is flat (horizontal) between $x=2$ and $x=4$, so $y$ does not change across that interval:
$$\text{rate}=\frac{\Delta y}{\Delta x}=\frac{0}{2}=0\ \text{cm/hour}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A: Rate of $2$ cm/hour from $x=2$ to $x=4$ would require a sloped segment, but the graph is flat there. Eliminated.
  • B: The greatest rate occurs where the curve is steepest, which is in the early hours, not between $x=4$ and $x=10$. Eliminated.
  • C: From $x=0$ to $x=2$ the slope is roughly constant, not increasing. Eliminated.
  • D: The flat segment from $x=2$ to $x=4$ gives a rate of $0$ cm/hour, which matches the graph.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
